steps.title: Spot the “But” Contrast
steps.content: The opening defines Nixon by his Watergate fame; the underlined sentence begins with “But,” shifting to positive accomplishments instead.

steps.title: Identify the Oversimplification
steps.content:
C captures what the underlined sentence accomplishes: it provides information (tribal sovereignty, health care reform) showing that the Watergate-focused perception is an oversimplification.

The others misidentify the function—
  • A invokes an explanation
  • B requires comparing two figures, but the sentence discusses Nixon's legacy rather than comparing Nixon with Obama
  • D references associates not mentioned in the underlined sentence


That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
